issues.description: Discord token is always set to undefined regardless of source config: `discordToken: data.channels?.discord?.enabled ? undefined : undefined`. This appears to be a bug - it should extract the token from the discord config object.
issues.suggestedFix: Change to: `discordToken: data.channels?.discord?.botToken` (or equivalent field name for the discord token in OpenClaw config)

issues.description: The USER.md merge logic reads the source content but doesn't actually use the merged content when writing to the target. When existing target USER.md has some entries and new lines exist, it still writes the original source content (not the merged content).
issues.suggestedFix: After the dedup check, build a merged content string (existing + new lines) and write that instead of the raw source content.

issues.description: Memory dedup is line-based (checking if each line exists in target), which may cause false negatives when individual lines match but context differs. A delimiter-based approach matching the contract spec would be more robust.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider using delimiter-separated entry parsing (e.g., entries separated by '---' or similar markers) for more accurate dedup.

issues.description: The allowlist target path uses `os.homedir()` directly instead of respecting the `HOME` environment variable override that the shell test uses. This means the allowlist migration always writes to the real home directory even in tests with custom HOME.

Unit tests are shallow - many tests verify argument parsing logic by checking that valid modes exist in an array, rather than testing the actual Migrator class behavior. The Migrator class is not directly imported and tested.
issues.suggestedFix: Import and instantiate the Migrator class directly in tests with mock source configs to test individual migration methods (migrateSoul, migrateChannels, etc.) without needing real filesystem source directories.

issues.description: Source detection tests create fixture files but only assert they exist, rather than actually testing the detectSources() function with the created fixtures.
issues.suggestedFix: Export detectSources from the script (or refactor to a separate module) and test it with custom HOME overrides to verify actual detection behavior.

issues.description: The summary.md and MIGRATION_NOTES.md files are written to the report directory even in dry-run mode, but the report path includes the target root which is the real ~/nano path by default. This is fine since reports are always written, but the summary.md mentions file paths that don't exist yet in dry-run mode.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider adding a note in the dry-run summary that file paths refer to what would be created.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The migration script uses `npx tsx scripts/migrate-to-nanocore.ts` for execution. The `npm run migrate` script was added to package.json. services.yaml test command uses `find tests -type f -name '*.test.ts'` which may miss shell-based tests like tests/test-migration.sh.
sharedStateObservations.suggestion: Add a separate command in services.yaml for running migration integration tests: `migration-test: bash tests/test-migration.sh`

sharedStateObservations.observation: The migration script implements a JSON5 comment stripper (removeComments function) and a trailing comma remover. These are general-purpose utilities that could be useful elsewhere in the codebase.
sharedStateObservations.suggestion: Document the removeComments and trailing comma handling utilities in library/ for future reference.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The script uses `maskSecret()` to prevent secrets from appearing in output. This is a good security pattern that should be documented.
sharedStateObservations.suggestion: Document the maskSecret pattern in library/ as a security convention.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The script's source detection logic checks 4 well-known paths: ~/.openclaw/, ~/.config/clawdbot/, ~/.moltbot/, ~/.hermes/. These are the canonical migration source paths.
sharedStateObservations.suggestion: Document the migration source paths in library/ for future reference.

summary: The migration-script-core feature implements a comprehensive CLI migration tool that handles source detection, dry-run/execute modes, 14 migration categories, conflict handling, backup creation, and structured reporting. The implementation is well-structured with a clean Migrator class, Zod schema validation for CLI args, and support for all 4 source systems (OpenClaw, Clawdbot, Moltbot, Hermes). The shell integration test (test-migration.sh) provides good end-to-end coverage passing all 6 test scenarios. Unit tests exist but are shallow - they mostly test argument parsing rather than actual migration logic. Two non-blocking bugs were found: Discord token extraction always returns undefined in OpenClaw parser, and USER.md merge doesn't use the deduplicated content when writing. Neither blocks the milestone since the core-script milestone focuses on the CLI framework, source detection, dry-run, and report generation - all of which work correctly.
